compiled_state.title: sprint-list-epics.sh --max-children=0 returns epics that have children
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description: Running sprint-list-epics.sh --max-children=0 returned epic 0d66-ceb6 (Unified Primary Ticket Model for Sprint Skill) which has 10 children. The script should only return epics with zero child tickets. Verified via: .claude/scripts/dso ticket deps 0d66-ceb6 shows children array with 10 entries. This causes brainstorm to present epics that already have decomposition as candidates for brainstorming.

comments.body: Additional finding: On re-run, the script fails entirely with 'line 71: File: unbound variable'. The root cause is stat -f %m (macOS syntax) on Linux — stat -f on Linux means 'filesystem mode' and interprets %m as a filename argument rather than a format specifier. With set -u (nounset), the arithmetic evaluation of the unexpected stat output causes an unbound variable error. The script ran successfully the first time in this session (likely using a cached sync marker from a prior session), but fails on subsequent runs when the marker file state changes.
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). severity=1, complexity=0, environment=0, intermittent=0.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). --max-children=0 behavior documented and tested to return only epics with zero children. Current behavior contradicts that intent.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation found filter logic is correct but stale data from swallowed git pull failures causes incorrect child_count. Root cause shared with c806-d740/d959-faa6 — the pull --rebase || true pattern. Fix: replaced pull --rebase with reset --hard origin/tickets in sprint-list-epics.sh, which ensures local branch always matches remote.
comments.author: Test
